Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Michael D. Milich 
Assistant City Attorney 
City of Modesto 
801 11th street 
P.O. Box 642 
Modesto, CA 
95353 
Dear Mr. Milich: 
July 18, 1988 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. 1-88-247 
We have received your letter concerning the new 
restrictions on publicly-funded mass mailings under Proposition 
73. 
Enclosed is a copy of a second interim advice letter which 
clarifies our June 16, 1988 advice. 
This second letter 
provides that city newsletters and other mailings may be sent 
at public expense so long as there is no reference to elected 
city officials except in the standard letterhead or logotype. 
This is only interim advice and may be changed in the future. 
The Commission will address this and other questions 
concerning the mass mailing restrictions in Proposition 73 at 
its July 26, 1988 meeting. 
We will include your name on our 
mailing list for information on the subject of mass mailings. 
DMG:KED:plh 
Enclosure 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel

Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Michael D. Milich 
Assistant city Attorney 
P.O. Box 642 
Modesto, CA 
95353 
Dear Mr. Milich: 
June 29, 1988 
Re: 
88-247 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on June 28, 1988 by the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact Kathryn Donovan, an attorney in 
the Legal ~ivision, directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to information needed. 
If your request is for 
informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 
18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours, 
Diane M. Griffiths

Ms. Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J Street, Suite 800 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, California 95804-0807 
801 
June 27, 1988 
Re: 
Prohibited Mass Mailings Under Proposition 73 
Dear Ms. Griffiths: 
CA 
We have received and reviewed your recent advice letter dated June 16, 1988, 
regarding mass mailings. 
While the advice letter clarified several areas of 
concern, we remain confused as to others. Generally, this confusion stems 
from what appears to be a conflict between items "g" and "i" on page 8 of your 
June 16, 1988, advice letter. 
Accordingly, we seek your advice with regard to the following matters: 
1. 
Every two months the City of Modesto sends out utility bills to 
all utility customers. 
Our understanding is that the bills themselves are not 
prohibited mass mailings. 
However, for some time the City has also included 
an informational insert with each utility bill. For your reference, the 
November-December 1987 and the March-April 1988 inserts are enclosed. 
Since 
these inserts are "reports containing items of general interest" which do not 
contain any reference to any elected official by "use of his or her name, 
signature or depiction in any photograph II , these inserts appear to be 
permissible mass mailings under item "g" set forth on page 8 of the advice 
letter. 
On the other hand, these inserts could be construed to be prohibited 
under item ";" set forth on page 8 of your advice letter because they may be 
interpreted as "unsolicited" "newsletters" which are sent to 200 or more 
members of the public. 


Ms. Diane M. Griffiths 
June 27, 1988 
Page two 
2. 
Enclosed is a copy of a brochure which was sent to every 
household in the City of Modesto to publicize the grand opening of the Modesto 
Centre Plaza in March of this year. Similar mass mailings are contemplated 
for future events at the Modesto Centre Plaza. 
Would such mass mailings be 
allowed under Item "g" as a "report containing items of general interest" so 
long as there is no reference to any elected official "by use of his or her 
name, signature or depiction in any photograph" or would such a mass mailing 
be prohibited under Item "i" as an "unsolicited" "substantially similar 
correspondence sent to 200 or more constituents or other members of the 
public"? 
3. 
Periodically the City of Modesto through a committee appointed 
by the City Council will conduct a survey to determine community concerns. A 
copy of such a survey that was developed by the Forward Modesto IV Committee 
and which was sent out in the Fall of 1986 is enclosed. 
Are future such 
surveys prohibited under paragraph "i" of page 8 of the advice letter because 
they are "unsolicited" "substantially similar correspondence sent to 200 or 
more constituents or members of the public"? 
4. 
Enclosed is a copy of the Modesto Police Department's 
Neighborhood Watch Newsletter. 
Once again, this report appears to be 
permitted under paragraph "g" of page 8 of the advice letter because it is a 
"report containing items of general interest" which is not being sent by an 
elected official or contain "any reference to an elected official by use of 
his or her name, signature or depiction in any photograph". 
On the other 
hand, the newsletter is routinely sent to 200 or more members of the public. 
It is unsolicited and therefore appears to be prohibited by paragraph "i" of 
page 8 of your advice letter. 
5. 
Enclosed is a copy the City of Modesto's Report to the People 
for the year 1988. 
The report was delivered to the City on June 16, 1988, by 
the printer. It was to be mailed to 500 people on Friday, June 17, 1988. 
The 
remaining 2,000 copies were to be advertised as available to the public. It 
appears to us that the report would now be prohibited under paragraph "g" of 
page 8 of your advice letter because it does contain the following: 
the 
signature of the Mayor, a photograph of the entire City Council, and the names 
of the Councilmembers. 
However, our question is whether or not this report 
could be disseminated even after the photograph of the City Council and the 
signature of the Mayor, and the names of the City Councilmembers were removed 
from the report. 
Once again, it appears that upon making these deletions the 
report would be a permissible mass mailing under paragraph "g" of page 8 of 
your advice letter but prohibited under paragraph "i" of page 8 of your advice 
letter because it is an "unsolicited" "substantially similar correspondence 
sent to 200 or more constituents or other members of the public". 


Ms. Diane M. Griffiths 
June 27, 1988 
Page three 
6. 
Enclosed is the 1988 Summer Activity Guide. 
The last page of 
the Guide contains a photograph of the Mayor and the names of the entire City 
Council as well as the Mayor's signature. It is our understanding that these 
portions of the Guide would have to be deleted for it to be a permissible mass 
mailing under paragraph "g" of page 8 of your advice letter. 
As before, our 
question is whether or not the Activity Guide would be prohibited under 
paragraph "i" of page 8 of the newsletter because it is an "unsolicited" 
"substantially similar correspondence sent to 200 or more constituents or 
other members of the public". 
The Activity Guide is distributed once a year 
by inserting it into the Modesto Bee newspaper which is then either mailed or 
hand delivered to subscribers in the City of Modesto.

UtJ~ 
MICHAEL D. MILICH 
Assistant City Attorney

)W INTEREST LOANS 
OW AVAILABLE 
nail business owners in Modesto may be eligible 
. a low interest loan from the Economic Develop· 
:ot Revolving Loan Fund (RLF) established 
:ently under the Community Development Block 
'ant (CDBG) program. 
The purpose of the RLF is to create or save jobs 
. low income Modesto residents, or to assist or 
ng a needed service to a low income area of the 
y. 
Applicants for a loan may be a sole proprietor-
p, partnership, or for-profit corporation with a 
siness in the City of Modesto. RLF loans may be 
:d for property acquisition, building construction, 
lovation, expansion, capital equipment, interim 
::tneing, working or venture capital. or a Small 
siness Administration 504 Companion Loan. 
itLF loans cannot be made to non·profit organiza· 
ns, for speculative building and renovation, or for 
estmenl'i and projects not related to direct job 
ation or retention, 
Jenerally, the interest rate will be near the cur-
t commercial bank prime rate, The RLF can pro-
e up to $50,000 as a companion loan or $25,000 
a single loan, with an equity share from the 
rower. 
Jusiness owners may submit applications at the 
using Program Office, 40 I H Street, Modesto. 
~ible applications will be sent to the Stanislaus 
lmty Economic Development Corporation 
EDCO) for financial analy~s. SCEIXO will then 
ke a recommendation to the City's Economic 
w'elopment Loan Pool Subcommittee, which will 
lrove or disapprove each loan and set the terms. 
;or more information, please call 577·5245. 
FREE CURBSIDE 
RECYCLING 
SERVICE 
EVERY 
WEEK 
Enjoy the convenience of weekly curbside recycl· 
ing pick-up. This free service l'i offered citywide to 
all residen ts. 
Place your recyclables in separate bags or boxes 
in a visible location at the curb in front of your home 
by 6 a.m. on your pick-up day. Boxes or other con-
tainers used for recyclables will be left for reuse. 
If you are a Gilton Solid Waste Management 
customer, your recycling pick-up day 'IS the same as 
your regular refuse collection. If you are a Modesto 
Disposal Service customer, caU Citizens Information 
Services at 577·5454 to find out your pick-up day. 
Please note that the curbside recycling pick·u)J day 
for all residents will be moved to the same day as 
refuse collection in the near future, Those affected 
will be notified when this change occurs. 
Recycling is easy; just prepare your recyclables as 
follows: 
• NEWSPAPER, place in grocery sack~ boxes, 
or tie in bundles. 
• CANS (aluminum and tin) rinse out, labels 
and lids are o.k. 
• GLASS (bottles and jars) , rinse out, labe~ and 
lids are o.k. 
• MOTOR OIL ' place in plastic gallon jugs and 
seal tightly. (Motor oil containers will not be 
returned). 
If you have other questions or comments about 
recycling, call Robert Van Orsow, recycling program 
coordinator, at 577·5493.

CONSERVATION TIPS FOR YOUR HOME 
Water convervation is everyone's responsibility. Here are a few tips to heJp you save water at home, Can 
you think of other~'! 
Limit your !.hower time. Showers use 5~IO gallons of water per minute. 
Use your wastebasket. rather than the toilet. for disposing of small items like facial tissue and cotton 
swabs. Each time you flush. J7 gallons of water are u!.Cd. 
Turn off the water after you wet your toothbrush and fill a cup for rinsing. 
Pool water in the sink. rather than letting it run. when shaving or washing. Shaving with the tap 
running uses 20 gallons of water, 
Run the dishwasher or washing machine only when you have a full load. 
Keep a bottle of water in the refrigerator for drinking 
Fix faucet leaks. Even a small leak can waste 7 gallo,ns a day. 
Do you have a good conservation tip or need a question answered? Call us at 577-5469. 
The City of Modesto. in cooperation with Post-Newsweek Cable. wants 
to provide you wi.th as much information as possible aoout services. activi 
ties. special program:. and events. 
You can watch the City Council meetings live every Tuesday on Cable Chan· 
nel 2. The Council meets at 4 p.m. on the first and third Tuesdays and at 
7:30 p.m. on the second and fourth Tuesdays of each month. 
The Mexlesto Planning Commission meetings are also broadcast live at 7:30 
p.m. on the first and third Mondays of each month. 
At 7 p.m. every Thursday. you can tune in to Channel 2 and see the Police 
Department Crime Prevention Show. 
During the second and fourth weeks of each month. Cable Channel 2 broad-
casts "Cityscape." a 30-minute show featuring a variety of City services. ac-
tivities and programs. Hosted by Modesto's pUblic information officer. 
"Cityscape" is broadcast at 6:30 p.m. Mondays. 4:30 p.m. Wednesdays and 
12:30 p.m. Saturdays during the second and fourth weeks of the month.

WHAT ABOUT ROCKWEllS? 
Although 70% of Modestans live in areas drained by rockwells, most of us know little about them. In fact. 
most of liS are not aware of them until the rains come -
and the streets flood. The following information 
may help you keep your neighborhood ro<..:kwells operating more satisfactorily. 
Q: What is a rock well? 
A rockwell is a hole that is excavated into the ground and filled with graded rock. It is usuaUy located 
in an area where storm waters are likely to collect. When stormwater collect,> in the area surrounding 
the rockwell, water enters the spaces between the particles of rock. The width and depth of the hole 
usually provide enough surface area for the water to he absorbed by ,the surrounding soil. 
Q: Why does my strcet always flood during tht! rains? 
Sin<.:c rock wells have a limited stomge capacity and fairly low absorption rates, ponding will occur 
in the street or intersection when rainfall exceeds 0,25 inches during a storm. Analysis of Modesto 
rainfall shows that the City will exceed that amount 14 times during a year. Therefore, ponding can 
be expected and may not always be a symptom of a malfunctioning rockwell. A rockwell is considered 
to be working reasonably well if the area drain5 within 24 to 36 hours after a storm. 
Q: What can I do? 
A rockwell can only be efficient if its walls are clear of obstructions; therefore it's important that 
these surfaces stay as clean as possible. City crews dean each rockwell on the average of once a year, 
You can help hy keeping litter, graflS clippings, motor oil, grease and other chemicals away from 
entrances to a rockwell. All of these items can clog a rockwell and create that "flooded" look. 
Q: Who do I caD with a question or concern about a rockwell? 
If you have additional Questions concerning the operation of a rockwell, please don't hesitate to telephone 
Citizens Information Services at 577-5254.

NEW ACCIDENT INVESTIGATION POLICY 
Starti ng in February, 1988, the Modesto 
Police 
Department 
is 
changing 
its 
accident 
investigation 
policy 
on 
property-damage-only 
motor 
vehicle 
collisions. 
Police officers will: 
1. Sti 11 
respond 
to 
the 
scene 
of 
an 
accident. 
2. We will 
insure that the roadway 
is 
clear and direct traffic as necessary. 
3. We will obtain tow trucks for those 
parties needing them. 
4. We 
will 
determine 
if any 
of 
the 
following violations have occurred: 
a. 
Driving under the influence. 
b. 
Driver's license violations. 
c. 
Proof of insurance violations. 
d. 
If any of the parties are injured. 
If the accident meets the above criteria, 
we will 
supply each driver a card 
to 
complete which 
includes 
name, 
address, 
and insurance company. 
We will remain at 
the scene until· drivers have completed 
and exchanged those cards. 
We wi 11 not i nvesti gate the cause of the 
accident 
and 
will 
only 
report 
the 
drivers' 
last 
names, 
vehicle 
license 
numbers, and type of collision (that is 
whether it is a broadside, rearend, etc.) 
and the speci fi c block number where the 
accident occurred. 
If when the offi cer is at the scene of 
the collision he determines that either 
of the drivers are in violation of Number 
4 above, a complete investigation will be 
done of the accident. 
Our change in policy became necessary to 
increase the amount of time officers will 
be 
available 
for 
major 
criminal 
investigations.

CRIME'S GOING DOWN! 
In 1987 The City of Modesto experienced a 
dramatic reduction of reported property 
crimes. 
The most significant reduction 
was 
in 
the 
area 
of 
residential 
burglaries. 
Reported 
residential 
burglaries 
were 
down 
23% 
over 
the 
reported burglaries in 1986. 
In 
February 1987, 
the Property Crimes 
Detail of the Modesto Police Department 
(whi ch 
has 
the 
respons i bi 1 i ty 
of 
investigating 
burglaries 
and 
grand 
thefts) under went a major change of 
direction. 
The investigations went from 
a 
reactive 
mode 
of 
investigation 
(investigating "cold burglaries") to a 
more 
proactive 
type 
of 
investigation. 
The 
direction 
of 
investigation 
was 
focused on narcotics violations. 
Historically 'it has been proven that the 
property crimes violators are for the 
most 
part 
also 
narcotics 
violators. 
Therefore, 
putting 
the 
narcotics 
violators 
in 
jail 
will 
reduce 
the 
reported property crimes. 
As a result of the change in tactics, the 
following data was collected. 
In 1987 there were 4,544 cases assigned 
to 
the 
Property Crimes 
Detail. 
This 
number is down considerably compared to 
1986, where 5,208 cases were assigned. 
In 1987 Detectives in the Property Crimes 
Detai 1 made 427 arrests for property and 
narcotics violations. 
They obtained and 
served 130 search warrants. 
As a resul t 
of these arrests and 
search warrants, 
$263,752 worth of stolen property was 
recovered. 
Also, 
$49,OOfr 
worth 
of 
illegal drugs and narcotics were seized. 
As a result of the change in direction of 
the Property Crimes Detail and efforts of 
the Field Operations Division and their 
Tactical Patrol Unit, Property Crimes in 
the Ci ty of Modesto are down and we hope 
tha t thi s trend wi 11 conti nue throughout 
1988.

NEW TRAFFIC LAWS 
There 
is 
new 
legislation 
concerning 
traffi claws that affect us 'all. 
Three 
new or amended laws are: 
1. 
22507.8 
evc 
(Ca1ifornia 
Vehic1e 
Code): 
Thi s 
bi 11 
adds 
to 
the 
existing 
handicapped 
parking 
laws. 
It now ill ega 1 to obs truct. block or 
otherwise 
bar 
access 
to 
disabled 
parking 
stalls. 
This 
applies 
to 
public and private owned lots 
2. 
23117 evC-Tethered Dogs: 
This is a 
new 
section 
which 
prohibits 
the 
transporti ng of a dog in the back of 
a truck or a space des i gned for a 
load unless: 
* The truck has racks 46 inches high. 
or 
* The truck bed is enclosed. or 
* The dog is cross tethered. or 
* The dog is in a cage. 
This section does not apply to: 
* Transportation of livestock. or 
* Dogs being transported for the 
purpose of ranching or farming. 
For 
information on 
how to set up the 
tetheri ng system contact the 1 oca 1 SPCA 
office. 
3. 
22526 CVC-Anti-Gridlock: 
This is a 
new 
section 
which 
makes 
it 
a 
violation for a vehicle to enter an 
intersection on a yellow or a green 
traffi c 
1 i ght 
when 
there 
is 
no 
possiblity 
for 
that 
vehicle 
to 
completely 
clear 
the 
intersection 
prior to the light turning red. 
The 
bill 
designates 
that 
if 
the 
intersection is posted the driver will 
recei ve a ci tati on for fail ure to obey a 
traffic sign. 
If the intersection is not 
posted the dri ver wi 11 
be ci ted for a 
parking violation. 
Duri ng heavy rush hour traffi c. be sure 
you can completely cross the intersection 
or you might receive a citation if your 
car is unable to clear the intersection 
when the traffic light turns red.

I n our September 1987 SCAN. we menti oned 
that the Police Department was going to 
start 
a 
new 
forgery 
investigation 
po 1 i cy.

results to date. 
In

1987. 
the 
Modesto 
Police 
Department instituted a policy of not 
investigating any fraudulent payroll or 
Government checks if the merchant had not 
obtained 
the 
right thumbprint of the 
person cashi ng the check. 
The pol icy was 
instituted because of the large volume of 
fraudu 1 ent 
checks 
that 
cou 1 d 
not 
be

identification of the passer. 
Since 
the 
thumbprint 
policy 
was 
instituted. 
the 
Police 
Department 
has 
experi enced a drama ti c reducti on 
1 n the 
number of reported forgeries 
involving 
payroll or Government checks. 
By July 1st of this year. 
the Police 
Department 
intends 
to 
extend 
the 
thumbpri nt pol icy to all fraudu 1 ent check 
cases. 
Help 
prevent forgery by requiring the 
person who gi ves you a check to place 
thei r ri ght thumbpri nt on the check. 
A 
thumbprint 
is 
positive 
identification 
that a criminal will not want to give you.

reviewed 
the

responsibility 
laws 
of the

be enforceable starting November 
27. 1987.

for other violations when 
they cannot 
show proof of 
insurance or 
proof of 
financial responsibility. 
Hha t 
does 
proof 
of 
fi nanci a 1 
responsibility and 
proof of 
insurance 
mean?

drivers 
of 
vehicles 
should have 
in their possession their 
insurance company name and pol icy number 
or 
a 
Sureity 
bond 
number. 
This 
information 
can 
be 
written 
on 
the 
registration card in the space provided.

embedding.first_500_words: • Fair Political Practices Commission Michael D. Milich Assistant City Attorney City of Modesto 801 11th street P.O. Box 642 Modesto, CA 95353 Dear Mr. Milich: July 18, 1988 Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. 1-88-247 We have received your letter concerning the new restrictions on publicly-funded mass mailings under Proposition 73. Enclosed is a copy of a second interim advice letter which clarifies our June 16, 1988 advice. This second letter provides that city newsletters and other mailings may be sent at public expense so long as there is no reference to elected city officials except in the standard letterhead or logotype. This is only interim advice and may be changed in the future. The Commission will address this and other questions concerning the mass mailing restrictions in Proposition 73 at its July 26, 1988 meeting. We will include your name on our mailing list for information on the subject of mass mailings. DMG:KED:plh Enclosure Sincerely, Diane M. Griffiths General Counsel t <- By: Kathryn E. Donovan Counsel, Legal Division 42R J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804,0807 • (9 I 6) 322~5660 • Fair Political Practices Commission Michael D. Milich Assistant city Attorney P.O. Box 642 Modesto, CA 95353 Dear Mr. Milich: June 29, 1988 Re: 88-247 Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act was received on June 28, 1988 by the Fair Political Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request, you may contact Kathryn Donovan, an attorney in the Legal ~ivision, directly at (916) 322-5901. We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to information needed. If your request is for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) You also should be aware that your letter and our response are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure. DMG:plh Very truly yours, Diane M. Griffiths General Counsel 42H J Street, ite HOO • P.(). Box HOi it Ms. Diane M. Griffiths General Counsel Fair Political Practices Commission 428 J Street, Suite 800 P. O. Box 807 Sacramento, California 95804-0807 801 June 27, 1988 Re: Prohibited Mass Mailings Under Proposition 73 Dear Ms. Griffiths: CA We have received and reviewed your recent advice letter dated June 16, 1988, regarding mass mailings. While the advice letter clarified several areas of concern, we remain confused as to others. Generally, this confusion stems from what appears to be a conflict between items "g" and "i" on page 8 of your June 16, 1988, advice letter. Accordingly, we seek your advice with
